description: A Python library that brings full bidirectional WAMP RPC and PubSub capabilities to FastAPI applications without requiring a separate WAMP router, using isolated peer-to-peer sessions per WebSocket connection.

userStories.title: Project scaffolding and package structure
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the project scaffolding so the library can be developed, tested, and published to PyPI.
acceptanceCriteria: pyproject.toml exists with: package name 'fastapi-headless-wamp', requires-python >= 3.12, dependencies (fastapi, starlette), dev dependencies (pytest, pytest-asyncio, httpx, uvicorn), Apache-2.0 license field
uv is the package manager; uv.lock is generated
.python-version file set to 3.14
Source layout: src/fastapi_headless_wamp/ with __init__.py, hub.py, session.py, protocol.py, serializers.py, service.py, types.py, errors.py (files can be stubs/empty for now)
LICENSE file with Apache 2.0 text and NOTICE file present
.gitignore for Python projects (venv, __pycache__, dist, .eggs, etc.)
__init__.py exports: WampHub, WampSession, WampService, rpc, subscribe, Serializer, JsonSerializer, and error classes (can be placeholder imports initially)
__init__.py configures logging: logging.getLogger('fastapi_headless_wamp').addHandler(logging.NullHandler())
Package is installable locally with uv pip install -e .
pyright or mypy configured in pyproject.toml
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: WAMP protocol constants and message types
userStories.description: As a developer, I need WAMP message type constants and message structures so the protocol layer can encode/decode messages correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: protocol.py defines IntEnum WampMessageType with all codes: HELLO=1, WELCOME=2, ABORT=3, GOODBYE=6, ERROR=8, PUBLISH=16, PUBLISHED=17, SUBSCRIBE=32, SUBSCRIBED=33, UNSUBSCRIBE=34, UNSUBSCRIBED=35, EVENT=36, CALL=48, CANCEL=49, RESULT=50, REGISTER=64, REGISTERED=65, UNREGISTER=66, UNREGISTERED=67, INVOCATION=68, INTERRUPT=69, YIELD=70

WAMP error URI string constants defined: wamp.error.no_such_procedure, wamp.error.invalid_argument, wamp.error.runtime_error, wamp.error.no_such_subscription, wamp.error.procedure_already_exists, wamp.error.canceled, wamp.error.system_shutdown, wamp.error.close_realm, wamp.error.goodbye_and_out
Message validation functions for each message type (validate correct array length and field types)
Type aliases or dataclasses for message structures in types.py
Unit tests in tests/test_protocol.py for message validation (valid and invalid messages)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Pluggable serialization system
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a serializer abstraction so JSON is the default but other formats can be added.
acceptanceCriteria: serializers.py defines Serializer protocol (typing.Protocol) with: encode(data: list) -> str | bytes, decode(data: str | bytes) -> list, protocol property (str), is_binary property (bool)
JsonSerializer implementation: protocol='json', is_binary=False, uses json.dumps/loads
Global serializer registry: register_serializer(serializer), get_serializer(protocol) -> Serializer, get_available_subprotocols() -> list[str]
JsonSerializer registered by default at module import
WAMP subprotocol string format: wamp.2.{protocol} (e.g. wamp.2.json)
Unit tests in tests/test_serializers.py for encode/decode with WAMP message arrays, registry lookup, subprotocol strings
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Exception hierarchy for WAMP errors
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a structured exception hierarchy so all WAMP errors are typed and consistent.
acceptanceCriteria: errors.py defines: WampError (base), WampProtocolError, WampInvalidMessage, WampNoSuchProcedure, WampNoSuchSubscription, WampRuntimeError, WampCallTimeout, WampCanceled, WampProcedureAlreadyExists
Each error class has a corresponding WAMP error URI attribute (e.g. WampNoSuchProcedure.uri = 'wamp.error.no_such_procedure')
Errors can carry args and kwargs for the WAMP ERROR message payload
Unit tests in tests/test_errors.py verifying error URIs and message formatting
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: WampSession core: WebSocket wrapper, send/receive, serialization
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a WampSession class that wraps a FastAPI WebSocket and handles serialized message I/O.
acceptanceCriteria: session.py defines WampSession class wrapping starlette.websockets.WebSocket
Constructor accepts WebSocket and Serializer
send_message(msg_list) serializes and sends via WebSocket (text for JSON, binary for binary serializers)
receive_message() -> list reads from WebSocket and deserializes
Async iterator support: async for message in session yields deserialized message lists
Session has unique integer ID (random, set during handshake), realm attribute, and is_open flag

Per-session state containers initialized: server_rpcs dict, client_rpcs dict, subscriptions dict, server_subscriptions dict, pending_calls dict (for calls TO client), request_id_counter (for server-initiated requests)
Proper cleanup method: clears all maps, rejects pending call futures, sets is_open=False
Uses logger = logging.getLogger(__name__) for all logging
Unit tests for send/receive round-trip, async iteration, cleanup
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: WAMP session handshake: HELLO/WELCOME/GOODBYE/ABORT
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the WAMP session lifecycle so clients can establish and teardown sessions properly.
acceptanceCriteria: Subprotocol negotiation: accept WebSocket with matching wamp.2.* subprotocol from Sec-WebSocket-Protocol header, select serializer accordingly

Receive HELLO from client, validate realm matches hub realm, generate unique session ID, send WELCOME with roles dict containing dealer features (progressive_call_results, call_canceling, caller_identification, call_timeout) and broker features (publisher_identification, publisher_exclusion, subscriber_blackwhite_listing)
Handle GOODBYE from client: send GOODBYE back with wamp.error.goodbye_and_out, close WebSocket cleanly
Handle ABORT: send ABORT, close WebSocket
If first message is not HELLO, send ABORT and close
Session ID is unique across all currently active sessions
Tests for: successful handshake, GOODBYE flow, ABORT on invalid first message, subprotocol negotiation
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: WampHub core and session tracking
userStories.description: As a developer, I need WampHub to orchestrate sessions and provide the central API surface.
acceptanceCriteria: hub.py defines WampHub class with realm parameter
WampHub.sessions -> dict[int, WampSession] mapping session_id to session
WampHub.session_count -> int property
Sessions added to hub.sessions on successful WELCOME, removed on disconnect/GOODBYE
Lifecycle callbacks: @wamp.on_session_open decorator and @wamp.on_session_close decorator, both receive WampSession
WampHub.handle_websocket(websocket) async method: accepts WebSocket, negotiates subprotocol, runs handshake, enters message dispatch loop, cleans up on disconnect
Message dispatch loop reads messages and routes by message type code to handler methods on session
asyncio-safe: hub state only mutated from the event loop
Integration test: FastAPI test client connects, completes handshake, disconnects; lifecycle callbacks fire correctly
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Server-side RPC registration and dispatch (standalone decorators)
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want to register Python functions as WAMP RPCs using decorators so clients can call them.
acceptanceCriteria: @wamp.register(uri) decorator stores async function as server-side RPC definition on hub
When client sends CALL for a registered URI, the handler is invoked with args/kwargs from the message
Handler return value sent back as RESULT [RESULT, request_id, details, [return_value]] or [RESULT, request_id, details, args_list, kwargs_dict]
Both async and sync functions supported; sync handlers run via asyncio.to_thread()
Exception in handler sends ERROR with wamp.error.runtime_error and exception details
CALL for non-existent URI sends ERROR with wamp.error.no_such_procedure
Request IDs from CALL correctly matched in RESULT/ERROR responses
call_timeout support: if client sends timeout in CALL options (milliseconds), server cancels handler after that duration and sends ERROR wamp.error.canceled
caller_identification: if client sends disclose_me: true in CALL options, handler can access caller session ID
Tests in tests/test_rpc.py for: success, sync handler, exception, non-existent procedure, timeout, caller_identification
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Class-based WampService with @rpc decorator
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want to group related RPCs into service classes with a URI prefix.
acceptanceCriteria: service.py defines WampService base class with optional prefix class attribute
@rpc(uri) decorator marks methods as RPC handlers; @rpc without args infers URI from method name
Full URI = prefix + '.' + uri (or prefix + '.' + method_name if no explicit URI)
Both async and sync methods supported (sync via asyncio.to_thread())
WampHub.register_service(service_instance) introspects service and registers all @rpc-marked methods
Service instance has access to hub via self.hub attribute (set during registration)
Tests in tests/test_service.py for: registration, invocation, prefix resolution, sync method
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Client REGISTER/UNREGISTER handling (client-side RPCs)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the server to handle REGISTER/UNREGISTER from clients so clients can expose RPCs the server can call.
acceptanceCriteria: Client sends REGISTER [REGISTER, request_id, options, procedure] -> server stores in that session's client_rpcs map with a unique registration_id, sends REGISTERED [REGISTERED, request_id, registration_id]
Client sends UNREGISTER [UNREGISTER, request_id, registration_id] -> server removes from that session's client_rpcs map, sends UNREGISTERED [UNREGISTERED, request_id]
UNREGISTER for non-existent registration -> ERROR wamp.error.no_such_procedure
Multiple clients can register the same URI independently (each in their own session, no conflict)
All client registrations cleaned up on session disconnect
Tests in tests/test_rpc_reverse.py for: register, unregister, non-existent unregister, independent registrations across sessions
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Server calling client-registered RPCs (session.call)
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want to call RPCs registered by a WAMP client so I can invoke client-side functions.
acceptanceCriteria: session.call(uri, args=None, kwargs=None, timeout=None) async method on WampSession
Looks up URI in that session's client_rpcs map; if not found raises WampNoSuchProcedure
Sends INVOCATION [INVOCATION, request_id, registration_id, details, args, kwargs] to client
Awaits YIELD from client -> returns result; awaits ERROR from client -> raises appropriate exception
Server-initiated request IDs use a separate counter from client-initiated IDs
Timeout support: if client doesn't respond within timeout seconds, reject future with WampCallTimeout
Pending call futures rejected on session disconnect
Tests for: successful call, client error response, timeout, non-existent procedure, disconnect during pending call
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Progressive call results (client calls server with receive_progress)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want server RPC handlers to send progressive results so long-running RPCs can stream intermediate data.
acceptanceCriteria: When client sends CALL with options.receive_progress = true, server RPC handler receives a _progress callback parameter
Calling await _progress(data) sends RESULT with options.progress = true to client
Handler's final return value sends RESULT without progress flag
If client does not request progressive results, _progress parameter is None
Request ID tracked throughout the progressive exchange
Tests in tests/test_progressive.py for: progressive results sent and received, final result, non-progressive call has _progress=None
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Progressive call results for server-to-client calls
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to receive progressive results when the server calls a client RPC.
acceptanceCriteria: session.call(uri, ..., receive_progress=True, on_progress=callback) sends INVOCATION with details.receive_progress = true
Progressive YIELD messages from client (with options.progress = true) trigger on_progress callback with the intermediate result
Final YIELD (without progress flag) resolves the call future with the final result
If on_progress is not provided but receive_progress is True, progressive yields are silently consumed
Tests for: progressive yields trigger callback, final yield resolves, no callback case
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Progressive call invocations (client streams input to server)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to support wampy.js progressiveCall() where the client sends multiple CALL messages with the same request ID.
acceptanceCriteria: Client sends multiple CALL messages with the same request_id and options.progress = true
Server accumulates these progressive input chunks and makes them available to the RPC handler (e.g. via an async iterator or queue)
Final CALL from client has options.progress absent or false, signaling end of input
RPC handler can iterate over input chunks as they arrive and produce result(s) after all input received
Tests for: multiple progressive CALLs accumulated, handler receives all chunks, final result sent
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Call cancellation (client cancels server call)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want clients to be able to cancel long-running RPCs via CANCEL messages.
acceptanceCriteria: Client sends CANCEL [CANCEL, request_id, options] for a pending CALL
Server cancels the running asyncio task for that handler (via task.cancel())
Server sends ERROR with wamp.error.canceled to client
If handler already completed before CANCEL arrives, CANCEL is ignored
options.mode support: 'skip' (stop waiting, let handler continue), 'kill' (cancel task), 'killnowait' (cancel task, don't wait for cleanup)
Tests in tests/test_cancellation.py for: cancel before completion, cancel after completion, cancel modes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Server cancels call to client
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the server to be able to cancel a pending call to a client RPC.
acceptanceCriteria: session.cancel(request_id) sends CANCEL [CANCEL, request_id, options] to the client
If client responds with ERROR wamp.error.canceled, the pending call future is rejected with WampCanceled
If client completes the call normally despite CANCEL, the result is returned as usual
Document known wampy.js limitation: callee side does not handle INTERRUPT, so client may not abort mid-execution
Tests for: cancel sent, client responds with error, client completes normally despite cancel
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: PubSub: client SUBSCRIBE/UNSUBSCRIBE handling
userStories.description: As a WAMP client, I want to subscribe to topics so I receive events the server publishes to my session.
acceptanceCriteria: Client sends SUBSCRIBE [SUBSCRIBE, request_id, options, topic] -> server stores in session's subscription map with unique subscription_id, sends SUBSCRIBED [SUBSCRIBED, request_id, subscription_id]
Client sends UNSUBSCRIBE [UNSUBSCRIBE, request_id, subscription_id] -> server removes from session's map, sends UNSUBSCRIBED [UNSUBSCRIBED, request_id]
UNSUBSCRIBE for non-existent subscription -> ERROR wamp.error.no_such_subscription
Subscriptions are per-session (isolated, not shared across clients)
All subscriptions cleaned up on session disconnect
Tests in tests/test_pubsub.py for: subscribe, unsubscribe, non-existent unsubscribe, cleanup on disconnect
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: PubSub: server publishes events to client
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want to publish events to a specific client session so subscribed clients receive them.
acceptanceCriteria: session.publish(topic, args=None, kwargs=None) async method sends EVENT to the client if it has subscribed to that topic
EVENT format: [EVENT, subscription_id, publication_id, details, args_list, kwargs_dict]
Publication IDs are unique and incrementing per session
If client is not subscribed to the topic, publish is a no-op (no error)
publisher_identification: optionally include details.publisher if subscriber requested it
Hub convenience: wamp.publish_to_all(topic, args, kwargs) publishes to all sessions where the client has subscribed to that topic
Tests for: publish to subscribed client, publish to unsubscribed (no-op), publish_to_all across multiple sessions
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: PubSub: client publishes, server receives
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want the server to subscribe to topics and handle events that clients publish.
acceptanceCriteria: @wamp.subscribe(topic) decorator registers server-side event handlers on the hub
When client sends PUBLISH on a topic with a registered server handler, the handler is invoked with event args/kwargs and the session
If options.acknowledge is true in client PUBLISH, send PUBLISHED [PUBLISHED, request_id, publication_id] back
publisher_exclusion: handle exclude_me option (trivial in peer-to-peer but parse correctly)
subscriber_blackwhite_listing: handle eligible/exclude options (trivial in peer-to-peer but parse correctly)
Also works in class-based services with @subscribe(topic) decorator on WampService methods
Tests for: client publish -> server handler invoked, acknowledge mode, class-based subscribe
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: FastAPI integration: manual handle_websocket and auto-mount router
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want both manual and automatic FastAPI integration patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: WampHub.handle_websocket(websocket) works as a complete async handler: accepts with subprotocol, runs handshake, enters message loop, cleans up on disconnect
handle_websocket blocks until client disconnects (the session is active and accessible via wamp.sessions during the connection)
WampHub.get_router(path='/ws') returns a pre-configured APIRouter with a WebSocket endpoint at the given path
app.include_router(wamp.get_router(path='/ws')) mounts the WAMP endpoint
Integration tests in tests/test_integration.py: connect via FastAPI test client using both patterns, verify handshake, call an RPC, disconnect
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Comprehensive integration test suite
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a full integration test simulating wampy.js message sequences to verify end-to-end behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Integration tests in tests/test_integration.py using FastAPI WebSocket test client
Test scenarios: full session lifecycle (connect, handshake, RPC call, PubSub subscribe+event, GOODBYE)
Test bidirectional RPC: server registers handler, client calls it; client registers handler, server calls it
Test progressive results flow end-to-end
Test call cancellation flow end-to-end
Test multiple concurrent WebSocket connections with isolated sessions
Test error paths: invalid messages, protocol violations, disconnects during pending calls
All tests pass with uv run pytest
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: README documentation and example files
userStories.description: As a user, I need a README and examples to get started quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md with: project description, installation instructions (pip install fastapi-headless-wamp), quick start showing both API styles (standalone decorator and class-based service), architecture diagram (peer-to-peer model), note about wampy.js compatibility
examples/basic_rpc.py: standalone decorator RPCs with FastAPI app
examples/class_service.py: class-based WampService example
examples/bidirectional.py: server calling client RPCs
examples/pubsub.py: subscribe/publish in both directions
All example files are valid Python that could run with uvicorn
Typecheck passes
